response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with genuine low-latency and high-throughput systems exposure (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby using DLang), but he does not meet the core minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ — his C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and full-stack product work rather than cloud compute platforms, and while he has mentoring and community-teaching credentials, he lacks demonstrated technical leadership of large-scale infrastructure or cloud platform teams.
strengths: Real high-performance, low-latency distributed systems experience (micro-services handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source authorship
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, courses, dojos)
concerns: Far short of the required 8 years of C++ experience; primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
No production cloud compute platform or cloud architecture ownership at scale
No formal technical-lead role managing cross-team priorities, deadlines, and deliverables on infrastructure projects
